summary:
Polycystic ovary syndrome primarily involves elevated Luteinizing Hormone (LH) leading to abnormal LH-to-FSH ratios; while minor prolactin elevations can occur secondarily, they do not characterize or diagnose the condition definitively.
The fundamental features of PCOS include persistent hyperlutenizing state resulting from defective negative feedback mechanism involving pituitary gland causing disproportionate increase over Follicular Stimulating Hormones thereby making it distinct from other conditions like hypoprolactinaemia where low PRL level would be more diagnostic clue pointing towards different etiologies such as Hypothalamus dysfunction etc... In contrast here focus lies upon understanding complex interplay between multiple hormones especially Sex Steroids whose dysregulation forms cornerstone underlying pathology responsible for diverse symptoms seen across spectrum ranging menstrual irregularities infertility hirsutism acne obesity metabolic disturbances cardiovascular risks among others necessitating comprehensive approach addressing multifactorial nature requiring individualized treatment plans tailored accordingly considering patient specific needs preferences lifestyle factors comorbidities genetic predispositions environmental influences psychological social aspects all together forming intricate tapestry defining overall management strategy aiming at optimizing quality life expectancy reducing morbidity mortality enhancing well being promoting healthy sustainable lifestyles empowering individuals taking charge their own healthcare journey toward achieving optimal outcomes desired most importantly ensuring dignity respect autonomy choice agency participation inclusion collaboration co-creation partnership shared decision-making transparent communication open dialogue fostering trust building rapport strengthening relationships nurturing connections creating supportive networks facilitating growth learning development transformation healing recovery restoration rejuvenation revitalisation The fundamental features of PCOS include persistent hyperlutenizing state resulting from defective negative feedback mechanism involving pituitary gland causing disproportionate increase over Follicular Stimulating Hormones thereby making it distinct from other conditions like hypoprolactinaemia where low PRL level would be more diagnostic clue pointing towards different etiologies such as Hypothalamus dysfunction etc...
